commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=84db594edfcdafa158466e0d239d88083eabae28)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a latest-assistant skip to `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` and updates focused thinking tests for older versus latest assistant turns.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main routes replay history through `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures`, and that function can modify the latest assistant turn. I did not run a live Anthropic session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor changes and real behavior proof before merge; automation should not take over while external proof remains a gate.

target.title: fix(thinking): preserve latest assistant message in stripInvalidThinkingSignatures (#85557)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes an `invalid_request_error` from Anthropic when `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` modifies thinking blocks in the latest assistant message. This error breaks sessions when thinking is toggled mid-session or models are switched.

## Problem

`stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` iterated over ALL assistant messages, including the latest turn. When it stripped invalid thinking signatures from the most recent assistant message, the next Anthropic API call rejected with:
```
invalid_request_error: messages.15.content.1: thinking or redacted_thinking blocks
in the latest assistant message cannot be modified.
```

The sibling function `dropThinkingBlocks` already had the correct guard — it finds `latestAssistantIndex` and skips that message. `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` was missing the same protection.

## Changes

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts`: Added `latestAssistantIndex` lookup to `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` and skip the latest assistant turn during invalid-signature stripping
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts`: Updated existing tests to use multi-turn histories so older turns are stripped while the latest is preserved; added new regression test for latest-message preservation

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Prevent invalid_request_error when stripInvalidThinkingSignatures touches latest assistant turn
Real environment tested: Local source checkout, Ubuntu Linux, Node 22
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts
  - pnpm format:check src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts
Evidence after fix: 29 tests pass, 0 failures; formatting check passes
Observed result after fix: Latest assistant message thinking blocks are preserved; older invalid signatures are still stripped
What was not tested: Live Anthropic API session with model switching mid-conversation

Fixes #85557
target.author: zhouhe-xydt
